assertion.text: Erdős Problem #713: declared status 'open'. Formalized: no. Is it true that, for every bipartite graph $G$, there exists some $\alpha\in [1,2)$ and $c>0$ such that\[\mathrm{ex}(n;G)\sim cn^\alpha?\]Must $\alpha$ be rational? Current best: Erd\H{o}s sometimes asked this in the weaker version with just\[\mathrm{ex}(n;G)\asymp n^{\alpha}.\]Erd\H{o}s \cite{Er67d} had initially conjectured that, for any bipartite graph $G$, $\mathrm{ex}(n;G)\sim cn^{\alpha}$ for some constant $c>0$ and $\alpha$ of the shape $1+\frac{1}{k}$ or $2-\frac{1}{k}$ for some integer $k\geq 2$. A simplified proof was given by F\"{u}redi and Gerbner \cite{FuGe21}, who extended it to a counterexample for all $k\geq 5$. Prize: $500. Tags: graph theory, turan number.
